get.description:
List emails across your mailboxes, newest first. Results carry a `snippet` preview but never the message body; fetch a single email to read it. Filter by mailbox, campaign, conversation, lead, read state, direction, folder, date, or full-text search.

Requires one of the following scopes: `emails:read`, `emails:all`, `all:read`, `all:all`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response’s `meta.nextCursor`.

parameters.name: mailbox
parameters.description: Filter by mailbox address, comma-separated. Addresses you do not own are ignored.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: Return only emails in this conversation. Pass a `threadId` exactly as returned - it is opaque.

parameters.name: isUnread
parameters.description: Filter by read state. Emails that are not part of a conversation have no read state and match neither `true` nor `false`.

parameters.name: folder
parameters.description: Return only emails whose conversation is in this folder - the same folders your team sees in the dashboard. `inbox`: campaign conversations someone replied to. `untracked`: conversations outside any campaign. `important`, `snoozed`, `archived`: as flagged in the dashboard - `important` spans the other folders. An email that belongs to no conversation is in no folder and matches none of these.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Full-text search over subject, preview text and sender address. Supports quoted phrases, `OR` and `-exclude`.

parameters.name: latestOfThread
parameters.description:
Return one email per conversation: the most recent one that matches the rest of your filters. A cheap conversation-style view without fetching whole threads.

The filters are applied first, then the newest survivor of each conversation is kept – so `latestOfThread=true&type=received` gives you the last email each contact sent you, even in conversations you have already replied to. An email that belongs to no conversation is a conversation of one and is always kept.

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: Order by received date. Defaults to newest first.

info.title: MailBeast Public API
info.description: Task-shaped REST API for campaigns, leads, deliverability, verification and lead discovery. Authenticate with an API key (`Authorization: Bearer mb_live_…`); the workspace is resolved from the key.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Create, list and revoke API keys programmatically.

tags.name: Campaigns
tags.description: Create, configure, launch and monitor email campaigns.

tags.name: Leads
tags.description: Add, read, update and bulk-manage a campaign’s leads.

tags.name: Workspace
tags.description: Read usage, subscription and workspace identity.

tags.name: Email Accounts
tags.description: Connect and manage the sending mailboxes your campaigns send from (SMTP/IMAP or native OAuth).

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: Engagement reporting – per campaign, over time, and across the workspace.

tags.name: Lead Finder
tags.description: Discover new companies and leads. List past searches and poll a running search’s progress.

code.description: Stable machine-readable code to switch on.

message.example: This API key is missing the required scope.

requestId.description: Correlation id - quote it in support requests.

details.description: Field-level validation messages (present on validation errors).

retryAfter.description: Seconds to wait before retrying (present on 429 responses).

scopes.description: Granted scopes (least-privilege). Must be a subset of the caller.

expiresAt.description: ISO-8601 expiry; omit for a non-expiring key.

keyPrefix.description: Masked prefix - the full secret is only shown once, at creation.

token.description: The full secret token. Store it now - it is never retrievable again.

nextPlannedAt.description: When the next step is scheduled to send; null if none pending.

sequenceProgress.description: Live sequence progress - included on the single-lead detail view.

hasMore.description: Whether more leads are available after this page.

nextCursor.description: Pass as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page; null when `hasMore` is false.

email.description: Lead email. Only structural validation here (a non-empty string within the length cap) - a malformed address is NOT a request-level 400 but a non-fatal row counted in `summary.invalid`, so one bad address never rejects the whole batch.

customFields.description: Custom fields - a flat map of string/number/boolean values. Each key must be lowercase letters, digits and underscores, starting with a letter, up to 64 characters (e.g. `fund_manager`); a key outside that form is rejected. The number of fields per campaign is capped, and each value is length-limited.

customFieldLabels.description: Display labels for custom-field columns (canonical slug → label). Applied only when a field is first defined on the campaign.

customFieldTypes.description: Data types for custom-field columns (canonical slug → text | url | number | date | image). Applied only when first defined.

leads.description: Leads to add (1-100). Each is deduplicated by email within the campaign, provider-detected, and counts toward the monthly-imports quota. Send one element to add a single lead.

duplicates.description: Skipped as duplicates (already in the campaign).

email.description: The email can only be changed while the lead has not been contacted yet.

customFields.description: Custom fields - each key must be lowercase letters, digits and underscores, starting with a letter, up to 64 characters (e.g. `fund_manager`); a key outside that form is rejected. The number of fields per campaign is capped, and each value is length-limited.

customFieldLabels.description: Display labels for custom-field columns (applied on first definition).

customFieldTypes.description: Data types for custom-field columns (text | url | number | date | image; applied on first definition).

status.description: Only user-settable statuses are allowed; platform-managed ones such as `email_sent` or `scheduled` are rejected. Reply-based statuses (`lead_replied_*`, `lead_converted`) require the lead to have already replied. `lead_lost` and `blacklisted` can be set at any time.

retentionDays.description: For soft-deleted leads, days before permanent purge.

search.description: Match on email / first name / last name / company.

ids.description: Target lead ids. Required for every operation except a filter-based `delete`.

filter.description: Select targets by filter instead of ids - supported ONLY for `delete`, and must contain at least one condition (an empty filter is rejected).

all.description: Delete EVERY lead in the campaign. Only valid for `delete`, and mutually exclusive with `ids` / `filter`. Required as an explicit opt-in for mass deletion - an empty `filter` will NOT delete all.

status.description: Required for `set_status`. Only user-settable statuses are allowed; platform-managed ones such as `email_sent` or `scheduled` are rejected. Reply-based statuses (`lead_replied_*`, `lead_converted`) require the lead to have already replied. `lead_lost` and `blacklisted` can be set at any time.

failed.description: Number of leads that could not be processed.

example: 2 lead(s) not found in this campaign

campaignId.description: The campaign this lead belongs to.

data.description: Up to 10 matches; one row per campaign the email appears in.

sendingMinutesStart.description: Send-window start as minutes from midnight (540 = 09:00).

sendingMinutesEnd.description: Send-window end as minutes from midnight (1020 = 17:00).

dailyLimit.description: Maximum emails per day for the campaign.

startDate.description: ISO-8601 start. Omit to start as soon as the campaign is launched (defaults to the creation time).

endDate.description: ISO-8601 end; omit to run indefinitely.

stopOnReply.description: Stop follow-ups to a lead once they reply.

subject.description: Email subject. Personalization tags like {{firstName}} are supported.

body.description: HTML body. Personalization tags like {{firstName}} are supported.

delayDays.description: Days to wait before this step (0 for the first email).

delayHours.description: Additional hours to wait before this step.

sequence.description: Inline email sequence steps, in send order. Omit for no sequence; when supplied it must have at least one step.

accounts.description: SMTP account ids to send from.

leads.description: Lead emails to add. The response summarizes how many were added. Up to 1000. A malformed address is NOT a request-level 400 - it is counted in the leads summary’s `invalid`, so one bad entry never rejects the create.

launch.description: Start sending immediately after the campaign is configured. Requires the `campaigns:lifecycle` scope in addition to `campaigns:write`.

color.description: Hex colour shown in the dashboard.

sequence.description: The email sequence steps - included on the detail view (GET /v1/campaigns/:id).

accounts.description: Attached SMTP account ids - included on the detail view.

tags.description: Tags on this campaign - the same ones the list returns, and the same ids `?tags=` filters by. Create and delete them in the dashboard. Campaign tags are a separate dictionary from mailbox tags, so the ids do not cross over.

launched.description: Whether the campaign was launched (true only when `launch: true` succeeded).

launchError.example: Campaign is not ready to launch: No leads added. Start it with POST /v1/campaigns/{id}/start.
launchError.description: Present only when `launch: true` was requested but the campaign could NOT be started. The campaign is still created (as a draft) - fix the issue and start it via the lifecycle endpoint. Absent on success.

tags.description: Tags on this campaign. Create and delete them in the dashboard - here they are read, and you can filter the list by them with `?tags=`. Campaign tags are a separate dictionary from mailbox tags, so the ids do not cross over.

hasMore.description: Whether more campaigns are available after this page.

nextCursor.description: Pass as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page; null when `hasMore` is false.

sequence.description: When supplied, REPLACES the full sequence with these steps (at least one). Omit to leave the sequence unchanged.

accounts.description: When supplied, REPLACES the attached SMTP account set.

isWithinSchedule.description: Whether the campaign is currently inside its sending window.

nextScheduleOpensAt.description: When the next sending window opens; null when in-window or not active.

variantId.description: The step variant to render. Take it from the campaign detail view.

accountId.description: The mailbox to send from. It must be one of yours and within its daily limit.

recipient.description: Where to send the test. A single address. It is refused if the address, or its domain, is on your blacklist.

personalization.description: Where merge variables come from. `dummy` fills placeholders. `lead` uses a real lead, named by `leadId`. There is no fallback: `lead` without `leadId` is rejected.

leadId.description: The lead whose data fills the merge variables. Required when `personalization` is `lead`, and must be omitted otherwise.

sent.description: The test email was handed to the mail server.

messageId.description: RFC 5322 Message-ID assigned by the sending server. `null` if the server did not return one.

limits.description: The five Email Outreach plan limits.

emailOutreach.description: Your Email Outreach plan usage - the five plan limits.

leadFinder.description: Your Lead Finder usage - a separate product with its own credit balance.

status.description: Subscription state: `active`, `on_hold`, or `inactive` (no active subscription - e.g. free plan).

currentPeriodEnd.description: When the current paid period ends - it renews then, or ends if `cancelAtPeriodEnd` is true. Null with no active subscription.

addons.description: Extra capacity bought as add-ons on this plan (already folded into your /v1/usage limits). Null when there are none.

tier.description: Lead Finder tier, or null if you have no Lead Finder subscription.

currentPeriodEnd.description: When the current Lead Finder paid period ends - its own cycle, independent of the Email Outreach plan.

emailOutreach.description: Your Email Outreach subscription - plan, billing cycle, trial, and add-ons.

leadFinder.description: Your separate Lead Finder subscription, or null if you have none.

organizationId.description: The organization resolved from the API key.

domainId.description: One of your workspace tracking domains. Attaching one enables open/click tracking for this mailbox. When omitted on connect, the workspace default is assigned; pass `null` for no tracking.

enabled.description: Enroll the mailbox in warmup. Defaults to true on connect.

strategy.description: `standard` and `aggressive` are fixed profiles (conservative / faster) with their own ramp, cap and reply rate. `custom` is what makes `maxPerDay`, `rampupEnabled`, `dailyRampup`, `randomizer` and `replyRatePercentage` take effect.

maxPerDay.description: Warmup emails per day, up to 30. Separate from `maxEmailsPerDay`, which caps CAMPAIGN sending only. `0` keeps warmup on but sends nothing. While the mailbox is still ramping up this is honoured on `custom` only (the preset strategies use their own cap), but once warmup reaches the steady maintaining stage it is the ceiling for EVERY strategy.

replyRatePercentage.description: Share of warmup emails that get a reply. `custom` strategy only; the presets use their own reply rate.

rampupEnabled.description: Grow the daily volume gradually (by `dailyRampup` each day) instead of sending `maxPerDay` from day one. `custom` strategy only.

dailyRampup.description: Emails added per day while ramping up. Read only when `rampupEnabled` is **true**. With ramp-up off the mailbox sends a flat `maxPerDay` every day and this is ignored. `custom` strategy only.

randomizer.description: Percent of warmup sends randomly skipped each day, so volume is not identical day to day. `custom` strategy only.

timezone.description: IANA timezone the warmup schedule runs in. Defaults to the workspace timezone.

displayName.description: Display name shown as the sender. Defaults to “First Last”.

smtpPassword.description: SMTP password. Stored encrypted; never returned.

imapPassword.description: IMAP password. Stored encrypted; never returned.

maxEmailsPerDay.description: Daily CAMPAIGN send cap for this mailbox. Warmup has its own budget (`warmup.maxPerDay`).

sendingGapMinutes.description: Minimum minutes between two sends from this mailbox, across all campaigns.

campaignSlowRamp.description: Ramp campaign volume up gradually when the mailbox joins a campaign.

signature.description: HTML signature appended to outgoing emails. Sanitized on save.

bcc.description: BCC every email from this mailbox to this address.

replyToAddress.description: Send replies somewhere other than the mailbox address. Omit to reply to the mailbox itself.

automationLanguage.description: ISO 639-1 language for auto-generated content (warmup bodies, replies).

warmup.description: Warmup config. Omit to accept the defaults (warmup ON).

accounts.description: Mailboxes to connect (1-50). Each is validated and created independently; the response reports which succeeded and which failed (e.g. an email already connected elsewhere).

strategy.description: Fixed preset (`standard` / `aggressive`), or `custom` to run on the tuning values below.

maxPerDay.description: Warmup emails per day. Separate from the campaign cap. Honoured while ramping up on `custom` only; from the maintaining stage on it caps every strategy.

replyRatePercentage.description: `custom` strategy only; the presets use their own reply rate.

dailyRampup.description: Emails added per day while ramping. Read only when `rampupEnabled` is true. `custom` strategy only.

randomizer.description: Percent of warmup sends randomly skipped each day. `custom` strategy only.

phase.description: Where the mailbox is in its warmup lifecycle. Null until warmup starts.

startedAt.description: When warmup began. Null if it never has.

poolAccess.description: Quality of the warmup pool this mailbox converses with. Set by your plan, read-only.

domainId.description: Tracking domain in use. Null means opens and clicks are not tracked for this mailbox.

responseCode.description: Numeric SMTP status returned by the provider.

overallStatus.description: Overall outcome of the mailbox checks.

provider.description: How the mailbox is connected: `custom` for any SMTP/IMAP mailbox (including Gmail/Outlook connected with an app password), or `google` / `microsoft` for a native OAuth connection.

status.description: Current state. `active` - sending. `inactive` - paused by you (or connected but not verified yet); nothing re-activates it on its own, call `resume`. `checking` - being verified. `failed` - we stopped it after repeated failures; see `statusMessage` for the provider’s reason, and `resume` re-checks it. `revoked` - the OAuth grant is gone; reconnect it.

maxEmailsPerDay.description: Daily CAMPAIGN send cap. Warmup is budgeted separately (`warmup.maxPerDay`).

sendingGapMinutes.description: Minimum minutes between two sends from this mailbox, across all campaigns.

campaignSlowRamp.description: Ramp campaign volume up gradually when the mailbox joins a campaign.

replyToAddress.description: Where replies are sent, when it differs from the mailbox address.

signature.description: HTML signature appended to outgoing emails.

statusMessage.description: Why the mailbox last failed, as reported by its provider. Null when it is healthy, and also null when the failure was on our side, since that is not a problem with your mailbox.

tags.description: Tags on this mailbox. Create and delete them in the dashboard - here they are read, and you can filter the list by them with `?tags=`.

imap.description: Null when no IMAP is configured.

index.description: Index of the failing account in the request array.

code.description: Branch on this, not on `error`. `mailbox_connected_elsewhere` - the address is connected in a different workspace; a mailbox can only live in one workspace at a time, so disconnect it there first. `mailbox_already_exists` - it is already connected in this workspace, nothing to do. `conflict` - any other conflict.

error.example: This mailbox is already connected to another MailBeast workspace. Disconnect it there before adding it here.
error.description: Human-readable reason, safe to show a user. Wording may change - match on `code` instead.

accounts.description: The mailboxes that were connected. They start `inactive` and are verified in the background - poll `GET /v1/accounts/{id}` for the outcome.

errors.description: The mailboxes that could not be connected. Each element is independent: the rest of the batch is still created.

nextCursor.description: Pass as `?cursor=` for the next page; `null` on the last page.

maxEmailsPerDay.description: Daily CAMPAIGN send cap. Warmup has its own budget (`warmup.maxPerDay`).

sendingGapMinutes.description: Minimum minutes between two sends from this mailbox, across all campaigns.

replyToAddress.description: Pass `""` to clear it and reply to the mailbox address again.

warmup.description: Warmup config. Only the keys you send are changed. Turning `enabled` on for a mailbox that has been paused resumes it where it left off.

replyToAddress.description: Pass `""` to clear it and reply to each mailbox address again, same as the single PATCH.

status.description: Enable (`active`) or disable (`inactive`) sending across the set. Mutually exclusive with `patch`.

patch.description: Settings/limits to apply to every account. Mutually exclusive with `status`.

id.description: The id you passed in `ids`, so a skip can always be matched back to the request.

email.description: Null when the id matches no mailbox in your workspace (`status: "not_found"`).

skipped.description: Mailboxes that were not eligible for this change.

error.description: Failure detail, or null when the check passed.

authorizationUrl.description: Open this consent URL in a browser so the mailbox owner can grant access.

state.description: Opaque handle. Pass it to the status endpoint to poll the result.

expiresInSeconds.description: How long the consent link / state stays valid.

accountId.description: The connected account id once `status` is `connected`; otherwise null.

threadId.description: Conversation this email belongs to. `null` when the email is not part of a conversation. An opaque identifier: pass it back as-is, do not parse or construct it.

messageId.description: RFC 5322 Message-ID as set by the sending server.

mailbox.description: The mailbox of yours that sent or received this email.

snippet.example: Thanks for the details, this works for us.
snippet.description: Short preview of the message. Always present in list results.

isUnread.description: Read state. `null` when the email is not part of a conversation and therefore has no read state, which is not the same as read.

nextCursor.description: Pass as `?cursor=` for the next page; `null` on the last page.

count.description: Number of unread emails matching the filters.

html.example: Thanks for the details, this works for us.
html.description: HTML body. `null` when the message has no HTML part.

text.example: Thanks for the details, this works for us.
text.description: Plain-text body. `null` when the message has no text part.

threadId.description: Conversation this email belongs to. `null` when the email is not part of a conversation. An opaque identifier: pass it back as-is, do not parse or construct it.

messageId.description: RFC 5322 Message-ID as set by the sending server.

mailbox.description: The mailbox of yours that sent or received this email.

snippet.example: Thanks for the details, this works for us.
snippet.description: Short preview of the message. Always present in list results.

isUnread.description: Read state. `null` when the email is not part of a conversation and therefore has no read state, which is not the same as read.

body.description: The message body. Returned only here, never in list results. A `null` field means that part was never stored for this message. It does NOT mean we failed to read it: if the stored body is unreadable right now, the call returns `503` instead, so a `200` always carries the real content.

id.description: The email that was queued for sending.

status.description: The email is queued for delivery. Poll the email to see it sent.

body.description: Your note above the forwarded message. The original message is quoted for you.

unreadCount.description: Emails left unread in the conversation.

clamped.description: true = your requested start was older than the plan retention window and was moved forward. The data you got is real but shorter than you asked for.

retentionDays.description: Days of history your plan retains.

total.description: Opens/clicks counted per email (bots excluded): the same email opened twice counts once, two different steps count twice.

uniqueLeads.description: Distinct leads who did this at least once.

failed.description: Sends that permanently failed before delivery.

leadsContacted.description: Distinct leads that received at least one email.

bounces.description: All bounces. Ask for `breakdown=bounce` to split hard/soft and by reason.

positiveLeads.description: Leads currently in a positive state (interested / meeting / converted).

conversionValue.description: Total value of those conversions, in your workspace currency.

totals.description: Rolled up across every campaign in the workspace.

interval.description: Bucket size actually used – may be coarser than requested for long windows.

positiveLeads.description: Leads currently in a positive state (interested / meeting / converted).

conversionValue.description: Total value of those conversions, in your workspace currency.

email.description: Sending account address. null when the account name is unavailable.

type.description: Who is responsible: sender (reputation/auth/content – fix your sending), recipient (bad address – clean your list), or unknown.

count.description: Unique messages that ended in this category.

percentage.description: Share of all bounces, percent. Category counts sum to `total`.

hard.description: Permanent failures (5xx) – the address is bad; drop it from the list.

soft.description: Temporary failures (4xx) – mailbox full, greylisting, throttling.

senderBounces.description: Bounces caused by the sender side (reputation, auth, content).

recipientBounces.description: Bounces caused by the recipient side (bad address, disabled mailbox).

unknownBounces.description: Bounces we could not attribute to either side.

query.description: The natural-language query this search was started from.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Terminal states are `completed`, `stopped`, `failed`; everything else is still running.

searchMode.description: Whether the search discovers companies or people.

maxResults.description: The requested discovery cap for this search.

completedAt.description: When the search reached a terminal state; null while still running.

hasMore.description: Whether more searches are available after this page.

nextCursor.description: Pass as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page; null when `hasMore` is false.

companiesProcessed.description: Of the discovered companies, how many have finished contact processing (crawled for emails/phones – including those with nothing found). Grows toward `companiesFound` as the run progresses.

discoveryFinished.description: Whether the discovery phase has finished (crawl/enrich may still run).

errorMessage.description: Failure reason when `status` is `failed`; otherwise null.

updatedAt.description: When the search row was last updated.

completedAt.description: When the search reached a terminal state; null while still running.
